title: Judicial Nominations Submitted to Senate for Confirmation
summary: Presidential nominations sent to the United States Senate for consideration and confirmation of candidates for federal judicial positions. The nominations include Angela Veronica Colmenero of Texas for United States District Judge for the Southern District of Texas, replacing retired Judge Lee H. Rosenthal; Daniel Desmond Domenico of Colorado for United States Circuit Judge for the Tenth Circuit; and Michael C. Martin of Michigan for United States District Judge for the Eastern District of Michigan. These nominations follow the standard constitutional process whereby the President nominates individuals to fill vacancies in the federal judiciary, and the Senate Judiciary Committee reviews the candidates before the full Senate votes on confirmation.

title: Executive Order Imposing Sanctions Related to Cuba
summary: This executive order authorizes the imposition of sanctions against individuals and entities determined to be responsible for repression in Cuba and activities assessed as threats to United States national security and foreign policy interests. The order invokes authority under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, the National Emergencies Act, and section 212(f) of the Immigration and Nationality Act of 1952 to implement these measures.
